plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero.
Reduce SPY to 20% to ease concentration.
Increase QQQI to 20% for income.
Trim CRWD to 10% to lower single name risk.
Add XLF to 10% for diversification.
Hold remaining cash buffer.
conservative.read: This plan aims for capital preservation with a target of ~1.5% per month. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, prioritizing stable income and broad index exposure to minimize drawdowns.

plan: Trim SOXL to 2% to reduce leverage.
Reduce SPY to 25% to address concentration.
Slightly trim QQQI and SMH.
Increase CRWD to 15% as it's a strong performer.
Add XLF to 10% for sector diversification.
Add TXN to 7% for quality tech exposure.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets ~2.5% per month, prioritizing risk-adjusted growth. It trims leverage and concentration while adding to strong performers and diversifying into financials and quality tech, aiming for steady growth with moderate risk.

plan: Increase SOXL to 10% to leverage tech upside.
Reduce SPY to 20% to free up capital.
Minimize QQQI and SMH to focus on growth.
Increase CRWD to 20% as a top performer.
Add ZS to 10% from the top scanner list.
Add ANET to 5% from the scanner list.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month, embracing higher potential returns with increased risk. It concentrates capital in high-scoring tech and adds leveraged ETFs, accepting larger potential drawdowns and the rebalancing decay of leveraged products.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and growth-focused single names (CRWD).
Sell QQQ and SMH to eliminate growth/tech concentration.
Maximize QQQI to 40% for income generation.
Add XLF to 20% for financial sector income.
Add SPY to 30% for broad market income exposure.
Hold remaining cash buffer for stability.
income.read: This plan prioritizes stable monthly income with a target of ~1% per month, focusing on capital preservation. It eliminates leverage and high-beta growth stocks, shifting entirely to income-generating ETFs and broad market exposure to provide consistent distributions.
